title: Unions and tagged values deterministic evidence bank

items.title: Unions and tagged values: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this C misconception? A union remembers which member was last written and prevents code from reading the wrong alternative.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. A C union is not self-tagging; maintain and validate a separate discriminator before interpreting the overlapping storage.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. A C union is not self-tagging; maintain and validate a separate discriminator before interpreting the overlapping storage.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned C correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: A C union is not self-tagging; maintain and validate a separate discriminator before interpreting the overlapping storage.
A union remembers which member was last written and prevents code from reading the wrong alternative.

answer.explanation: A C union is not self-tagging; maintain and validate a separate discriminator before interpreting the overlapping storage.

items.title: Unions and tagged values: executable application
items.prompt: Read tag i or d and a value, store it in the matching member of a tagged union, and print int N or double N.NN without reading an inactive member.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards, security, and assessment review.

hints: Begin with the declared input and exact output contract, then implement the named language facet without relying on undefined behavior.

feedback.correct: The reference-aligned visible and hidden deterministic checks passed in the pinned isolated runtime.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic check failed; inspect input validation, boundary behavior, and the named language rule without exposing hidden cases.

criteria.description: Compiles in the pinned runtime and passes every visible and hidden exact-output case.
Uses the target C or C++ language facet with declared bounds and without an out-of-scope workaround.

privateAuthorNotes: AI-assisted and human-unreviewed: formal exam eligibility is prohibited until independent technical and assessment approval.
Reference verification uses the digest-pinned local container with network=none, no package installation, a read-only source mount, and deterministic stdin/stdout.
Executable facet: tagged union with active-member discipline

answer.explanation: The reference solution exercises tagged union with active-member discipline under the bounded deterministic contract; it remains an AI-assisted draft pending human review.
